title: Tandoori Chicken Charcoal Grilled

seo.title: Tandoori Chicken Recipe (Charcoal Grilled) - Authentic Indian
seo.metaDescription: Master the art of authentic Tandoori Chicken with our charcoal grilled recipe. Experience smoky Indian flavors at home with easy step-by-step instructions. Perfect for dinner!

h2Headings: What is Tandoori Chicken Charcoal Grilled?

seo.imageAlt: Authentic Tandoori Chicken Charcoal Grilled recipe, marinated chicken cooked on a charcoal grill
seo.imageCaption: Experience the smoky, authentic flavors of our Tandoori Chicken, perfectly charcoal grilled for an unforgettable Indian dinner.

description: Tandoori Chicken Charcoal Grilled is a quintessential Indian dish, featuring chicken marinated in yogurt and a vibrant blend of aromatic spices, then cooked to smoky perfection over a charcoal grill. The high heat of the grill locks in the juices, creating tender, flavorful chicken with a beautiful charred exterior. This recipe brings the authentic taste of Indian tandoor right to your backyard, promising a delicious and memorable meal.

ingredients.item: Chicken drumsticks and thighs, bone-in, skinless

instructions.title: Prepare the Chicken
instructions.text: Wash the chicken pieces thoroughly and pat them completely dry with paper towels. Make deep slits or scores on the chicken pieces to allow the marinade to penetrate well. Place them in a large bowl.

instructions.title: First Marinade
instructions.text: To the chicken, add 1 tablespoon of lemon juice and 1 teaspoon of salt. Rub it all over the chicken pieces and let it rest for 15 minutes. This step helps tenderize the chicken and infuse initial flavor.

instructions.title: Second Marinade
instructions.text: In a separate bowl, combine the thick yogurt, ginger-garlic paste, remaining 2 tablespoons of lemon juice, Tandoori Masala, Kashmiri red chili powder, turmeric powder, cumin powder, coriander powder, garam masala, remaining 1/2 teaspoon of salt, mustard oil, and red food color (if using). Mix well to form a smooth paste.

instructions.title: Marinate the Chicken
instructions.text: Add the chicken pieces to the second marinade, ensuring each piece is fully coated. Cover the bowl with plastic wrap and refrigerate for at least 4 hours, or preferably overnight, for best flavor penetration.

instructions.title: Prepare the Charcoal Grill
instructions.text: About 30-40 minutes before cooking, prepare your charcoal grill. Arrange the charcoal for direct medium-high heat. The grill is ready when the coals are covered with a light gray ash. Lightly oil the grill grates to prevent sticking.

instructions.title: Grill the Tandoori Chicken
instructions.text: Place the marinated chicken pieces directly over the hot coals. Grill for 6-8 minutes per side, turning occasionally, until the chicken is cooked through, tender, and has beautiful char marks. Baste with any leftover marinade or a little oil during the last few minutes if desired. Ensure internal temperature reaches 165°F (74°C).

instructions.title: Rest and Serve
instructions.text: Once cooked, remove the chicken from the grill and let it rest for 5 minutes. Garnish with fresh chopped coriander, onion rings, and lemon wedges. Serve hot with mint chutney, naan bread, or rice.

tips: For truly authentic flavor, use full-fat plain yogurt and mustard oil in the marinade; the mustard oil adds a pungent depth that is characteristic of Tandoori dishes.
Marinating the chicken for a minimum of 4 hours, and ideally overnight, is crucial for the flavors to deeply penetrate the meat, resulting in a more tender and flavorful Tandoori Chicken.

To achieve a smoky flavor even without a charcoal grill, you can finish the chicken under a broiler or in a very hot oven, then add a charcoal smoke effect by placing a small, hot charcoal piece in a small bowl with a teaspoon of ghee next to the cooked chicken inside a covered pot for a few minutes.
article: Tandoori Chicken Charcoal Grilled is a beloved Indian Dinner Recipes dish enjoyed across Indian. This authentic recipe brings traditional flavors to your kitchen.
